summary:
DNETs commonly present as well-defined lesions in the temporal lobe, often linked to epilepsy, making them suitable candidates for radiosurgery. Stereotactic radiosurgery has been shown to effectively control seizures in such cases and is widely recognized as a valid, clinically applicable option within modern neurooncologic management.  

\boxed{A}
